thread.section_title: Mom, 26, charged after ‘accidentally baking her one-month-old baby in oven instead of putting her crib’ in Missouri

author: NewsBreak Original
text:
By Israel Salas-Rodriguez ,
7 hours ago THE mother who admitted to fatally shooting her two young boys, claiming WiFi had manipulated her, has been sentenced to two life terms in prison.
A courtroom in Kentucky erupted in applause after a judge sentenced Tiffanie Lucas , 32, to life imprisonment on Friday for the deaths of her children , Maurice Baker Jr., 6, and Jayden Howard, 9.
Tiffanie Lucas pleaded guilty to fatally shooting her two sons in October WDRB Little Jayden Howard (left) and Maurice Baker Jr. (right) were found with gunshot wounds in their home in Kentucky in November 2023 Facebook/Celine Tiffanie Ann Jayden (left) and Maurice (right) were stepbrothers and had different fathers Facebook/Celine Tiffanie Ann Lucas , who was chained from the wrists and ankles, sat next to her defense attorney unfazed as family members of her children ripped her apart in impact statements.
“You killed two good kids, you don’t deserve public defenders, you don’t deserve the sheriff’s around you,” the father of Maurice said as he stared daggers through Lucas from the witness stand.
Jayden’s grandmother, Regina Rowan, fought back tears as she told Lucas she could’ve given the boys to the family instead of “taking her grandchild from her.”
“You know we loved Jayden and Maurice. You know Jayden was the only thing we had left,” Rowan fumed as tears streamed down her face.
“And you took him from us. You know I had already lost his dad. And then my other son. And then you took Jayden.
I want you to suffer just like I’m suffering right now.
Regina Rowan, Jayden's grandmother “You done ripped our lives apart, and you know that you could’ve gave them to us and we would’ve took care of them.
“But you have been selfish. My heart is in pieces, and you know that you have always had a place to drop them off.
“You took [Jayden] from me. And I hurt every day, every second of the day, I hurt, and I hope they give you two life sentences without the possibility of parole.
“I want you to suffer just like I’m suffering right now. They did not deserve that. You took my little man from me, and we all love them so much, Jayden and Maurice.”
Bobbie Baker, Maurice’s aunt, slammed Lucas, “I will never forgive you. I will be there at every parole hearing.”
Richard Lawniczak, Lucas’ defense attorney, claimed the mother was suffering from severe mental illness in the days and moments before she killed her boys.
“No one could’ve imagined Tiffanie could have hurt those children,” Lawniczak said.
“Tiffanie tried to get help as her mind was drowning in mental illness.”
‘SHEER HORROR’ Despite her attorney’s pleas, Judge Rodney Burress took into account the severity of Lucas’ crimes and sentenced her to two life sentences, one for each murder.
“I cannot imagine the sheer horror these children must have experienced seeing their mother with a gun,” Burress said before he imposed sentencing.
The courtroom broke out in applause as the judge handed down the sentencing.
Lucas stood emotionless as correction officers escorted her out of the courtroom.
The father of Maurice Baker Jr. ripped Tiffanie Lucas in his impact statement, calling her a ‘monster’ WLKY Tiffanie Lucas tried to tell police that the shooting of her children was an accident, a detective previously said in court Facebook/Celine Tiffanie Ann Sheriff’s officials found the boys with gunshot wounds to the head inside their home in Shepherdsville, Kentucky, on November 8, 2023 WDRB VILE ACT Little Jayden and Maurice were found with gunshot wounds inside their home in Shepherdsville, Kentucky, about 20 miles south of Louisville, on November 8, 2023.
When Bullitt County sheriff officials arrived at the home, deputies found Lucas collapsed on her neighbor’s driveway.
The children were rushed to the hospital, where they died of their injuries.
At the time, Lucas told investigators she was “in such a bad spot” and had allegedly been “manipulated” by Facebook and WiFi to kill her children , according to court documents.
Bullitt County Detective Richard Beahl previously said in court that Lucas “made statements she was being manipulated through Facebook, through the internet or through WiFi… into doing what she did.”
Lucas also told Beahl that the incident was an “accident.”
“I asked Ms. Lucas if she meant to hurt her children. She indicated that it was an accident,” Beahl added.
Leigha Jade, a former friend of Lucas, had previously claimed the mother was going down a “dark path.”
“She was on a bad path back then & I was too but the point is even then knowing her I would have never imagined this,” Jade posted in a Facebook post days after the incident.
“This is heartbreaking & so demonic. This makes me so mad.”
If you or someone you know needs help or support for their mental health, text “STRENGTH” to the Crisis Text Line at 741-741 to be connected with a certified crisis counselor.
